pages.full_text:
We camped next to k.Orne & later in the day drove near it in Suisse Normande. Suisse Normande is about the size of the Black Country. Hills & water. We bought creme boule, cider, apple aperitif, cheese, bread, avocados, yogurt at Thury Harcourt, & changed travellers cheques. It's Misty.
	We found a beautiful, secluded valley in the middle of the Suisse Normandes, next to a large oxbow, which encloses land with a hill, houses, cemetery, fields, & a gap only [illegible] 120 metres wide. (a high ridge & house). __Rouvrou__ is the village. The only restaurant is shut on Wednesdays, so we are having both meals in the van. After lunch we dozed. A man was strumming in a corner of the campsite, which was very annoying, because all around it was quiet. We went for a short, strenuous walk after tea - along old old roads & looked at the view. When we came back I had a hot shower. Thu. Oct 10[?s]
	It is one of the prettiest campsites that I have ever seen. We left & drove to Arromanches. We came to see the amaz[ing]
pages.notes: Page top is cut off in scan; no handwritten page number visible. Date annotation 'Thu. Oct 10' appears in purple/pink ink at bottom of text block, likely a later annotation. October 10 fell on a Thursday in 1996 and 2002 within the volume's range (1989-2003); year cannot be confirmed from this page alone. The text continues mid-sentence at the bottom ('the amaz[ing]') onto a subsequent page. The asterisk-like marks after [illegible] in '120 metres wide' line may be ditto/decoration marks. 'k.Orne' may be 'r. Orne' (River Orne).
Page is blank apart from handwritten page number '47' in the top-right corner. The left edge of this image shows overflow/gutter text from the left page — not transcribed again here.
